title: Uses and Abuses of the Atomic Displacement Parameters in Structural Biology.
abstract: B-factors determined with X-ray crystallographic analyses are commonly used to estimate the flexibility degree of atoms, residues, and molecular moieties in biological macromolecules. In this chapter, the most recent studies and applications of B-factors in protein engineering and structural biology are briefly summarized. Particular emphasis is given to the limitations in using B-factors, in order to prevent inappropriate applications. It is eventually predicted that future applications will involve anisotropically refined B-factors, deep learning, and data produced by cryo-EM.
